title: HPE SimpliVity Backup Job Reliability and Deduplication Efficiency
dataSources: PRIMARY (custom HTTPS HEC poller to index hci): OAuth2 POST https://{ovc-fqdn}/api/oauth/token; paginated GET https://{ovc-fqdn}/api/backups with fields id,name,virtual_machine_id,virtual_machine_name,state,type,created_at,expiration_time,sent,unique_size_bytes,saved_size_bytes,policy_id,policy_name,cluster_id,cluster_name,application_consistent,size_bytes, limit=2000, since= cursor, following next links; GET /api/policies; GET /api/virtual_machines with fields id,name,policy_id,policy_name,state,cluster_id,cluster_name,size_bytes,created_at; GET /api/omnistack_clusters for dedupe_ratio, compression_ratio, efficiency_ratio when present, physical_capacity_bytes, used_capacity_bytes. Sourcetypes simplivity:backup:job (5 min), simplivity:policy, simplivity:vm, simplivity:cluster:efficiency (30 min). SECONDARY (optional): Splunk_TA_nix sourcetype simplivity:cli:vm_policy_audit from omnistack-cli when REST throttles. TERTIARY (optional): Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder sourcetype vmware:vc:event for backup-adjacent vCenter tasks.
app: Custom HEC poller for HPE SimpliVity OmniStack REST API; (optional) Splunk_TA_nix for omnistack-cli side-collector; (optional) Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder for vCenter event corroboration.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.20 — HPE SimpliVity backup job reliability, RPO versus target minutes, VM policy gaps, dedupe drift. INDEX hci. SOURCES simplivity:backup:job,simplivity:vm,simplivity:cluster:efficiency. LOOKUP simplivity_policy_owner.csv. FREEZE change_freeze_until. TUNABLES failure_pct drift_pct target_rpo_minutes. OWNER hci_backup_ops.")`
| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:backup:job" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
| eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
| eval cluster_name=tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, ""))
| eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
| eval policy_name_evt=tostring(coalesce(policy_name, policyName, ""))
| eval vm_id=tostring(coalesce(virtual_machine_id, virtualMachineId, ""))
| eval job_state=lower(tostring(coalesce(state, job_state, backup_state, "")))
| eval created_t=coalesce(tonumber(created_at, 10), _time)
| eval is_fail=if(match(job_state, "(fail|error|abort|cancel)"), 1, 0)
| eval is_succ=if(match(job_state, "(complete|success|commit|finish|done|sent)"), 1, 0)
| eval is_skip=if(match(job_state, "skip"), 1, 0)
| sort 0 cluster_id policy_id created_t
| streamstats window=60 global=f avg(is_fail) as failure_rate_trend by cluster_id policy_id
| stats count as total_jobs_24h sum(is_fail) as failed_jobs_24h sum(is_skip) as skipped_jobs_24h dc(vm_id) as vm_touched latest(failure_rate_trend) as failure_rate_trend latest(policy_name_evt) as policy_name_evt latest(cluster_name) as cluster_name max(eval(if(is_succ=1, created_t, null()))) as last_success_epoch by cluster_id policy_id
| eval failure_pct=if(total_jobs_24h>0, round((100*failed_jobs_24h)/total_jobs_24h, 2), 0)
| eval oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes=if(isnotnull(last_success_epoch), round((now()-last_success_epoch)/60, 2), null())
| eval row_kind="policy"
| append
    [| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:vm" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval cluster_name=tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, ""))
      | eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
      | eval vm_name=tostring(coalesce(name, vm_name, virtual_machine_name, virtualMachineName, ""))
      | eval vm_id=tostring(coalesce(id, virtual_machine_id, virtualMachineId, ""))
      | where len(policy_id)=0 OR policy_id="null" OR lower(policy_id)="none"
      | eval row_kind="vm_gap"
      | eval total_jobs_24h=0 | eval failed_jobs_24h=0 | eval skipped_jobs_24h=0 | eval failure_pct=0 | eval failure_rate_trend=null()
      | eval oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes=null() | eval last_success_epoch=null() | eval policy_name_evt=""
      | eval policy_gap_reason="no_policy_assignment" | eval last_succ=null() | eval age_min=null() ]
| append
    [| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:vm" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval cluster_name=tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, ""))
      | eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
      | eval vm_name=tostring(coalesce(name, vm_name, virtual_machine_name, virtualMachineName, ""))
      | eval vm_id=tostring(coalesce(id, virtual_machine_id, virtualMachineId, ""))
      | where len(policy_id)>0 AND policy_id!="null" AND lower(policy_id)!="none"
      | join type=left max=0 cluster_id vm_id
          [| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:backup:job" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
            | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
            | eval vm_id=tostring(coalesce(virtual_machine_id, virtualMachineId, ""))
            | eval job_state=lower(tostring(coalesce(state, "")))
            | eval is_succ=if(match(job_state, "(complete|success|commit|finish|done|sent)"), 1, 0)
            | stats max(eval(if(is_succ=1, coalesce(tonumber(created_at, 10), _time), null()))) as last_succ by cluster_id, vm_id ]
      | join type=left max=0 cluster_id policy_id
          [| inputlookup simplivity_policy_owner.csv
            | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
            | eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
            | eval target_rpo_minutes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_rpo_minutes, targetRpoMinutes, "1440")), 10)
            | fields + cluster_id, policy_id, target_rpo_minutes ]
      | eval age_min=if(isnotnull(last_succ), round((now()-last_succ)/60, 2), null())
      | where isnull(last_succ) OR ((isnotnull(age_min)) AND (isnotnull(target_rpo_minutes)) AND (target_rpo_minutes>0) AND (age_min>target_rpo_minutes))
      | eval row_kind="vm_backup_gap"
      | eval policy_gap_reason="no_success_in_policy_window"
      | eval total_jobs_24h=0 | eval failed_jobs_24h=0 | eval skipped_jobs_24h=0 | eval failure_pct=0 | eval failure_rate_trend=null()
      | eval oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes=age_min | eval last_success_epoch=last_succ | eval policy_name_evt="" ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id policy_id
    [| inputlookup simplivity_policy_owner.csv
      | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
      | eval policy_name_csv=tostring(coalesce(policy_name, policyName, ""))
      | eval owner_team=tostring(coalesce(owner_team, ownerTeam, ""))
      | eval target_rpo_minutes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_rpo_minutes, targetRpoMinutes, "1440")), 10)
      | eval target_min_dedupe_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_dedupe_ratio, targetMinDedupeRatio, "0")), 10)
      | eval change_freeze_until=tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, changeFreezeUntil, ""))
      | fields + cluster_id, policy_id, policy_name_csv, owner_team, target_rpo_minutes, target_min_dedupe_ratio, change_freeze_until ]
| eval policy_display=coalesce(policy_name_csv, policy_name_evt, policy_id)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:cluster:efficiency" earliest=-7d@d latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval dedupe_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(dedupe_ratio, dedupeRatio, "0")), 10)
      | eventstats avg(dedupe_ratio) as dedupe_ratio_baseline by cluster_id
      | sort 0 cluster_id - _time
      | dedup cluster_id
      | eval current_dedupe_ratio=dedupe_ratio
      | eval dedupe_ratio_drift_pct=if((dedupe_ratio_baseline>0), round((((current_dedupe_ratio - dedupe_ratio_baseline) / dedupe_ratio_baseline) * 100), 2), null()) ]
| fillnull value="" change_freeze_until policy_gap_reason policy_display
| fillnull value=0 target_min_dedupe_ratio
| eval rpo_breach=case((row_kind="vm_backup_gap"), 1, (row_kind="policy"), if((isnotnull(oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes)) AND (isnotnull(target_rpo_minutes)) AND (target_rpo_minutes>0) AND (oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes>target_rpo_minutes), 1, 0), 1=1, 0)
| eval below_min_dedupe=if((isnotnull(current_dedupe_ratio)) AND (target_min_dedupe_ratio>0) AND (current_dedupe_ratio<target_min_dedupe_ratio), 1, 0)
| eval cft1=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cft1)) OR (isnotnull(cft2)) OR (isnotnull(cft3)) OR (isnotnull(cft4)), if((now()<coalesce(cft1, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0)), 1, 0), 0)
| eval sev0=case(((failure_pct>=40) OR ((failure_pct>=20) AND (rpo_breach=1))), "critical", ((failure_pct>=10) OR (rpo_breach=1)), "high", (row_kind="vm_backup_gap"), "high", (row_kind="vm_gap"), "warning", ((below_min_dedupe=1) AND (isnotnull(dedupe_ratio_drift_pct)) AND (dedupe_ratio_drift_pct<=-15)), "high", ((isnotnull(dedupe_ratio_drift_pct)) AND (dedupe_ratio_drift_pct<=-10)), "warning", ((failure_pct>0) OR (below_min_dedupe=1)), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND ((sev0="critical") OR (sev0="high")), if((sev0="critical"), "warning", "info"), sev0)
| table cluster_id cluster_name policy_id policy_display row_kind vm_id vm_name total_jobs_24h failed_jobs_24h skipped_jobs_24h failure_pct failure_rate_trend oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes target_rpo_minutes rpo_breach last_success_epoch current_dedupe_ratio dedupe_ratio_baseline dedupe_ratio_drift_pct target_min_dedupe_ratio below_min_dedupe severity_bucket freeze_active owner_team policy_gap_reason
description: Monitors HPE SimpliVity OmniStack backup jobs, policy-level recovery-point freshness against contracted minutes, virtual machine coverage against SimpliVity policies, and cluster deduplication ratio drift using OVC REST telemetry forwarded over HEC, then unifies governance metadata from simplivity_policy_owner.csv with optional VMware corroboration for operational context.
value: Backup misses on HCI are restore misses; this use case turns SimpliVity pass-fail, skipped work, recovery-point age, missing policy bindings, and deduplication regression into ticketable rows within the polling window so operations can rerun jobs, fix assignments, or refresh capacity plans before audit windows and before restores are tested under pressure.
implementation: Stand up the OAuth-capable poller with five-minute backup ingests and thirty-minute inventory ingests, publish simplivity_policy_owner.csv, index the four sourcetypes into hci, paste the SPL into a fifteen-minute saved search, then route high and critical policy rows to the backup bridge and vm_gap rows to virtualization owners.
visualization: Failed-jobs single value by severity, RPO-breach table with policy_display and oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes, orphan VM single value and detail table, dedupe_ratio and dedupe_ratio_drift_pct trend by cluster_id, with optional vmware:vc:event correlation panel when the VMware TA is licensed.
evidence: Scheduled saved search ss_uc_19_2_20_simplivity_backup_rpo_dedupe_v1 with export-on-trigger to a restricted evidence index or ticket attachment, retaining row_kind, policy_display, failure_pct, rpo_breach, dedupe_ratio_drift_pct, freeze_active, and owner_team for sampled audits.
knownFalsePositives: Planned retention pruning can delete many expired backups in one window, which raises visible job counts without implying new backup failures. Ephemeral templates and deliberate exclusions for automation sandboxes can look like orphan VMs until you tag them out. Cluster expansion or large ingest of incompressible data can lower dedupe_ratio until cold data re-optimizes, which looks like drift without a policy bug. OVC failover can yield a transient empty backups page in one poll bucket; verify the following sample before opening a severity-one bridge.
hardware: HPE SimpliVity OmniStack on HPE ProLiant DL380 Gen10, Gen11, and Gen12, DL360 Gen10 and Gen11, and DL325 Gen10 and Gen11 nodes with the OmniStack accelerator PCIe card (SVT-EQ class) for inline processing, in small, medium, and large factory-configured node counts depending on cluster generation, including rack-and-stack deployments separate from SimpliVity 380 H series appliances when your procurement path uses customer-supplied ProLiant.
exclusions: SimpliVity 380 Hyperscale-X stacks that publish a different REST namespace than OmniStack on ProLiant are out of scope here and need their own sourcetype contracts. This UC is not HPE Alletra or Nimble primary storage telemetry; pair UC-19.2.22 for dHCI array paths instead. Pure VMware estates without licensed SimpliVity and OmniStack 4.x clusters that never expose efficiency_ratio are excluded unless you remap fields. Single-node ROBO SimpliVity without a remote destination is excluded when your program explicitly accepts no off-box backup target.
grandmaExplanation: We watch HPE SimpliVity backups so problems surface soon, not only in the morning report. We compare the last good backup age to the minutes you agreed per policy, list missing policies or late VM runs, and track deduplication drift when storage efficiency shifts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

You are implementing Splunk coverage for HPE SimpliVity OmniStack backup job telemetry produced by a customer-owned HTTPS poller against the OmniStack Virtual Controller (OVC) REST surface at https://{ovc-fqdn}/api/ with OAuth2 client credentials for a service account modeled on svtcli with Cluster_Admin or an explicitly documented read-only OmniStack auditor role that still lists backups, policies, virtual machines, and cluster efficiency endpoints without write authority. Splunk ingests four sourcetype families into index hci (recommended) or a customer index documented in the same change request as the HEC token: simplivity:backup:job for every paginated GET /api/backups poll that emits one JSON event per backup or per page summary as you design, but fields must include stable identifiers for cluster_id, policy_id, virtual_machine_id, state, type, created_at, expiration_time, sent, unique_size_bytes, saved_size_bytes, and size_bytes where present. simplivity:policy for GET /api/policies on a 30-minute cadence with id, name, cluster_id, frequency_minutes, retention_minutes, application_consistent, destination_id, and consistency_type flattened into indexed fields. simplivity:vm for GET /api/virtual_machines with id, name, state, cluster_id, cluster_name, size_bytes, policy_id, policy_name, and created_at. simplivity:cluster:efficiency for GET /api/omnistack_clusters with id, name, dedupe_ratio, compression_ratio, efficiency_ratio when your release returns it, physical_capacity_bytes, used_capacity_bytes, and member host identifiers if you choose to flatten them into multivalue fields for correlation only.

You will publish lookups/simplivity_policy_owner.csv in the same Splunk app that owns the transforms and expose it through transforms.conf as simplivity_policy_owner or reference it by filename in searches. The required columns are cluster_id, policy_id, policy_name, owner_team, target_rpo_minutes, target_min_dedupe_ratio, and change_freeze_until. cluster_id and policy_id together are the primary join keys from the SimpliVity API id fields; policy_name is human text for dashboards and may differ from live API labels but should be maintained when operations renames a policy because the UC joins on ids first. owner_team is a short routing label such as hci_backup_ops or win_vm_lifecycle that becomes the alert recipient mapping in Step 4. target_rpo_minutes is the contracted maximum age in minutes for the newest successful backup row for VMs governed by that policy, which can be tighter than frequency_minutes when business rules demand proof of recovery points inside the scheduled window even if the UI default suggests otherwise. target_min_dedupe_ratio is a decimal baseline such as 2.5 representing the minimum acceptable deduplication ratio for a cluster row after you normalize API units, used only when you also ingest cluster efficiency events; leave 0 or empty if your release omits dedupe_ratio. change_freeze_until accepts ISO-8601 timestamps with zone, UTC Zulu, or date-only, and the SPL in this use case demotes severities during active freeze windows while still surfacing rows at informational severity so evidence exists without pager storms.

Assign a dedicated HEC token named hec_simplivity_ovc_primary (example; document your exact Splunk HEC stanza name in git) scoped to index hci with acknowledgment enabled for backup job traffic, or separate tokens per sourcetype family if your security team requires blast-radius isolation. The poller must implement a since cursor strategy: persist the last successfully ingested created_at or server-side opaque cursor returned by the backups list API, and on each five-minute cycle call GET /api/backups with limit=2000 and since= set to the saved cursor minus a small overlap window your team agrees (for example 120 seconds) so transient duplicates are handled with dedup in Step 2 rather than missing events during clock skew. When the API returns a next link, follow it until exhausted before advancing the cursor. If the indexer fleet was unavailable for hours, your poller should detect large negative wall-clock gaps and widen the re-pull window to at least 24 hours on the next healthy cycle while emitting a sidecar sourcetype event simplivity:poller:health with rehydration=true so operators know counts will spike.

OAuth token acquisition must POST /api/oauth/token with grant_type password or client_credentials as your OmniStack build documents, capturing access_token and expires_in. Refresh proactively at eighty percent of lifetime or on first HTTP 401, with capped exponential backoff and jitter so an OVC under patch does not stampede the token endpoint. Log authentication failures through HEC as structured events without embedding secrets. Store client_id, client_secret, and the svtcli-equivalent password in your corporate vault; the only credential on the poller host should be a short-lived file permissioned to the service account running systemd or Windows Service.

Optional secondary: Splunk_TA_nix on management hosts can ship omnistack-cli scripted output to sourcetype simplivity:cli:vm_policy_audit when REST rate limits require per-host pulls from each OmniStack VC; normalize fields to match simplivity:vm so the searches can fall back with coalesce. Optional tertiary: Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380 family, commonly Splunk_TA_vmware in deployments) can land vmware:vc:event for tasks and alarms that mention snapshot, backup, or datastore events that correlate to SimpliVity plugin activity; this UC does not require VMware for math, but correlation lowers ambiguity when Splunk shows backup noise and vCenter shows simultaneous storage maintenance.

Why this is not UC-19.2.6 and not UC-19.2.31: UC-19.2.6 governs replication factor and storage policy compliance across multiple HCI vendors; it does not read SimpliVity backup outcomes. UC-19.2.31 governs asynchronous protection-domain replication and remote-site lag on Nutanix; this file governs SimpliVity backup pass-fail, policy assignment coverage, backup-age RPO versus contract minutes, and OmniStack deduplication efficiency drift, which is a different failure mode than Nutanix replication throughput. UC-19.2.22 is sibling HPE dHCI and Alletra array latency; SimpliVity OmniStack backup APIs live on the OVC and are product-specific even when rack layouts share ProLiant Gen11 or Gen12 classes.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

Create a Splunk wrapper app TA_splunk_simplivity_hec with local/inputs.conf granting a global HEC stanza, and local/props.conf defining the four sourcetypes with SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false, TRUNCATE=0 or 1048576 per your JSON sizes, TIME_PREFIX targeting epoch or ISO fields you emit, and KV_MODE=json when each HEC post is a single JSON object. Example inputs.conf fragment:

```ini
[http://hec_simplivity_ovc_primary]
disabled = 0
indexes = hci
token = <paste from Splunk HEC settings>
useACK = 1
```

On a systemd-managed Linux collector or an automation worker in Kubernetes, schedule backup poll every five minutes and policy, VM, cluster efficiency polls every thirty minutes. Prefer a service unit with Restart=always over bare cron for the five-minute loop; reserve cron only for long-running inventory if you split processes.

Props.conf fragment:

```ini
[simplivity:backup:job]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 1048570
KV_MODE = json

[simplivity:policy]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = json

[simplivity:vm]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = json

[simplivity:cluster:efficiency]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = json
```

transforms.conf for the owner lookup:

```ini
[simplivity_policy_owner]
filename = simplivity_policy_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0
```

Sample Python 3 pseudocode showing OAuth refresh and paginated backup fetch with a since cursor persisted to /var/lib/splunk_simplivity/backup_since.state guarded with fcntl locks:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, os, ssl, time, urllib.parse, urllib.request
OVC = os.environ["OVC_BASE"]  # https://ovc.example.local/api
USER = os.environ["SVT_USER"]
PASSWORD = os.environ["SVT_PASSWORD"]
HEC = os.environ["SPLUNK_HEC_URL"] + "/services/collector/event"
HEC_TOKEN = os.environ["SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN"]
STATE = "/var/lib/splunk_simplivity/backup_since.state"

def oauth_token(ctx):
    body = json.dumps({"grant_type": "password", "username": USER, "password": PASSWORD}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(OVC + "/oauth/token", data=body, method="POST",
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json", "Accept": "application/json"})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=60) as resp:
        tok = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
    return tok["access_token"], float(tok.get("expires_in", 1200))

def hec_send(st, event):
    payload = json.dumps({"sourcetype": st, "index": "hci", "source": "simplivity_poller",
        "time": int(time.time()), "event": event}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(HEC, data=payload, method="POST",
        headers={"Authorization": "Splunk " + HEC_TOKEN})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ssl.create_default_context(), timeout=60) as resp:
        assert resp.status == 200

def read_since():
    try:
        return open(STATE).read().strip()
    except FileNotFoundError:
        return ""

def write_since(v):
    tmp = STATE + ".tmp"
    open(tmp, "w").write(v)
    os.replace(tmp, STATE)

def fetch_backups(ctx, token, since):
    url = (OVC + "/backups?fields=id,name,virtual_machine_id,virtual_machine_name,state,type,"
           "created_at,expiration_time,sent,unique_size_bytes,saved_size_bytes,policy_id,policy_name,"
           "cluster_id,cluster_name,application_consistent,size_bytes&limit=2000")
    if since:
        url += "&since=" + urllib.parse.quote(since, safe="")
    rows = []
    next_url = url
    while next_url:
        req = urllib.request.Request(next_url, headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token, "Accept": "application/json"})
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=120) as resp:
            doc = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
        rows.extend(doc.get("objects", doc.get("results", [])))
        next_url = doc.get("next")
    return rows

def main():
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    token, _exp = oauth_token(ctx)
    since = read_since()
    try:
        backups = fetch_backups(ctx, token, since)
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        if e.code == 401:
            token, _exp = oauth_token(ctx)
            backups = fetch_backups(ctx, token, since)
        else:
            raise
    max_created = since
    for b in backups:
        hec_send("simplivity:backup:job", b)
        c = str(b.get("created_at", ""))
        if c and c > max_created:
            max_created = c
    if max_created:
        write_since(max_created)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

PowerShell 7 uses Invoke-RestMethod for token and backups, stores the cursor in ProgramData, and posts HEC events with Invoke-WebRequest; enforce TLS 1.2, validate certificates in production, and never log bearer tokens.

Operational gates before enabling alerts: prove at least one non-zero count for each sourcetype in index hci with metadata queries, confirm OAuth rotation with a forced 401 drill, confirm cursor overlap replays at most a low single-digit percent duplicate backup ids, and validate that policy_id on backup events matches a row from simplivity:policy in the same wall-clock hour.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL below as ss_uc_19_2_20_simplivity_backup_rpo_dedupe_v1 with cron schedule star-slash-fifteen across minutes (every fifteen minutes) and dispatch.earliest_time of minus sixty minutes on the saved search object while the interior search still uses earliest=-24h@h latest=@h on backup arms so accounting stays stable. Alert when severity_bucket is high or critical for row_kind=policy or row_kind=vm_backup_gap, and treat row_kind=vm_gap warning rows as ticket-only unless your CMDB marks the cluster as tier-one. Use alert.suppress on cluster_id, policy_id, and row_kind for twenty minutes. Failed job bursts and policy-level RPO breach ladder map to high or critical per the case statement; vm_backup_gap reflects per-VM contract miss and maps to high; vm_gap reflects assignments missing a policy and maps to warning; dedupe drift maps to warning until it crosses below_min_dedupe with deep negative drift, which maps to high.

Example savedsearches.conf:

```ini
[ss_uc_19_2_20_simplivity_backup_rpo_dedupe_v1]
search = <paste spl from spl field>
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -60m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
alert.track = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.fields = cluster_id,policy_id,row_kind
alert.suppress.period = 20m
```

Paste SPL (canonical copy of the repository spl field):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.20 — HPE SimpliVity backup job reliability, RPO versus target minutes, VM policy gaps, dedupe drift. INDEX hci. SOURCES simplivity:backup:job,simplivity:vm,simplivity:cluster:efficiency. LOOKUP simplivity_policy_owner.csv. FREEZE change_freeze_until. TUNABLES failure_pct drift_pct target_rpo_minutes. OWNER hci_backup_ops.")`
| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:backup:job" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
| eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
| eval cluster_name=tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, ""))
| eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
| eval policy_name_evt=tostring(coalesce(policy_name, policyName, ""))
| eval vm_id=tostring(coalesce(virtual_machine_id, virtualMachineId, ""))
| eval job_state=lower(tostring(coalesce(state, job_state, backup_state, "")))
| eval created_t=coalesce(tonumber(created_at, 10), _time)
| eval is_fail=if(match(job_state, "(fail|error|abort|cancel)"), 1, 0)
| eval is_succ=if(match(job_state, "(complete|success|commit|finish|done|sent)"), 1, 0)
| eval is_skip=if(match(job_state, "skip"), 1, 0)
| sort 0 cluster_id policy_id created_t
| streamstats window=60 global=f avg(is_fail) as failure_rate_trend by cluster_id policy_id
| stats count as total_jobs_24h sum(is_fail) as failed_jobs_24h sum(is_skip) as skipped_jobs_24h dc(vm_id) as vm_touched latest(failure_rate_trend) as failure_rate_trend latest(policy_name_evt) as policy_name_evt latest(cluster_name) as cluster_name max(eval(if(is_succ=1, created_t, null()))) as last_success_epoch by cluster_id policy_id
| eval failure_pct=if(total_jobs_24h>0, round((100*failed_jobs_24h)/total_jobs_24h, 2), 0)
| eval oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes=if(isnotnull(last_success_epoch), round((now()-last_success_epoch)/60, 2), null())
| eval row_kind="policy"
| append
    [| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:vm" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval cluster_name=tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, ""))
      | eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
      | eval vm_name=tostring(coalesce(name, vm_name, virtual_machine_name, virtualMachineName, ""))
      | eval vm_id=tostring(coalesce(id, virtual_machine_id, virtualMachineId, ""))
      | where len(policy_id)=0 OR policy_id="null" OR lower(policy_id)="none"
      | eval row_kind="vm_gap"
      | eval total_jobs_24h=0 | eval failed_jobs_24h=0 | eval skipped_jobs_24h=0 | eval failure_pct=0 | eval failure_rate_trend=null()
      | eval oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes=null() | eval last_success_epoch=null() | eval policy_name_evt=""
      | eval policy_gap_reason="no_policy_assignment" | eval last_succ=null() | eval age_min=null() ]
| append
    [| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:vm" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval cluster_name=tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, ""))
      | eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
      | eval vm_name=tostring(coalesce(name, vm_name, virtual_machine_name, virtualMachineName, ""))
      | eval vm_id=tostring(coalesce(id, virtual_machine_id, virtualMachineId, ""))
      | where len(policy_id)>0 AND policy_id!="null" AND lower(policy_id)!="none"
      | join type=left max=0 cluster_id vm_id
          [| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:backup:job" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
            | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
            | eval vm_id=tostring(coalesce(virtual_machine_id, virtualMachineId, ""))
            | eval job_state=lower(tostring(coalesce(state, "")))
            | eval is_succ=if(match(job_state, "(complete|success|commit|finish|done|sent)"), 1, 0)
            | stats max(eval(if(is_succ=1, coalesce(tonumber(created_at, 10), _time), null()))) as last_succ by cluster_id, vm_id ]
      | join type=left max=0 cluster_id policy_id
          [| inputlookup simplivity_policy_owner.csv
            | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
            | eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
            | eval target_rpo_minutes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_rpo_minutes, targetRpoMinutes, "1440")), 10)
            | fields + cluster_id, policy_id, target_rpo_minutes ]
      | eval age_min=if(isnotnull(last_succ), round((now()-last_succ)/60, 2), null())
      | where isnull(last_succ) OR ((isnotnull(age_min)) AND (isnotnull(target_rpo_minutes)) AND (target_rpo_minutes>0) AND (age_min>target_rpo_minutes))
      | eval row_kind="vm_backup_gap"
      | eval policy_gap_reason="no_success_in_policy_window"
      | eval total_jobs_24h=0 | eval failed_jobs_24h=0 | eval skipped_jobs_24h=0 | eval failure_pct=0 | eval failure_rate_trend=null()
      | eval oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes=age_min | eval last_success_epoch=last_succ | eval policy_name_evt="" ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id policy_id
    [| inputlookup simplivity_policy_owner.csv
      | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval policy_id=tostring(coalesce(policy_id, policyId, ""))
      | eval policy_name_csv=tostring(coalesce(policy_name, policyName, ""))
      | eval owner_team=tostring(coalesce(owner_team, ownerTeam, ""))
      | eval target_rpo_minutes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_rpo_minutes, targetRpoMinutes, "1440")), 10)
      | eval target_min_dedupe_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_dedupe_ratio, targetMinDedupeRatio, "0")), 10)
      | eval change_freeze_until=tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, changeFreezeUntil, ""))
      | fields + cluster_id, policy_id, policy_name_csv, owner_team, target_rpo_minutes, target_min_dedupe_ratio, change_freeze_until ]
| eval policy_display=coalesce(policy_name_csv, policy_name_evt, policy_id)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| search index=hci sourcetype="simplivity:cluster:efficiency" earliest=-7d@d latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval dedupe_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(dedupe_ratio, dedupeRatio, "0")), 10)
      | eventstats avg(dedupe_ratio) as dedupe_ratio_baseline by cluster_id
      | sort 0 cluster_id - _time
      | dedup cluster_id
      | eval current_dedupe_ratio=dedupe_ratio
      | eval dedupe_ratio_drift_pct=if((dedupe_ratio_baseline>0), round((((current_dedupe_ratio - dedupe_ratio_baseline) / dedupe_ratio_baseline) * 100), 2), null()) ]
| fillnull value="" change_freeze_until policy_gap_reason policy_display
| fillnull value=0 target_min_dedupe_ratio
| eval rpo_breach=case((row_kind="vm_backup_gap"), 1, (row_kind="policy"), if((isnotnull(oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes)) AND (isnotnull(target_rpo_minutes)) AND (target_rpo_minutes>0) AND (oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes>target_rpo_minutes), 1, 0), 1=1, 0)
| eval below_min_dedupe=if((isnotnull(current_dedupe_ratio)) AND (target_min_dedupe_ratio>0) AND (current_dedupe_ratio<target_min_dedupe_ratio), 1, 0)
| eval cft1=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cft1)) OR (isnotnull(cft2)) OR (isnotnull(cft3)) OR (isnotnull(cft4)), if((now()<coalesce(cft1, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0)), 1, 0), 0)
| eval sev0=case(((failure_pct>=40) OR ((failure_pct>=20) AND (rpo_breach=1))), "critical", ((failure_pct>=10) OR (rpo_breach=1)), "high", (row_kind="vm_backup_gap"), "high", (row_kind="vm_gap"), "warning", ((below_min_dedupe=1) AND (isnotnull(dedupe_ratio_drift_pct)) AND (dedupe_ratio_drift_pct<=-15)), "high", ((isnotnull(dedupe_ratio_drift_pct)) AND (dedupe_ratio_drift_pct<=-10)), "warning", ((failure_pct>0) OR (below_min_dedupe=1)), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND ((sev0="critical") OR (sev0="high")), if((sev0="critical"), "warning", "info"), sev0)
| table cluster_id cluster_name policy_id policy_display row_kind vm_id vm_name total_jobs_24h failed_jobs_24h skipped_jobs_24h failure_pct failure_rate_trend oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes target_rpo_minutes rpo_breach last_success_epoch current_dedupe_ratio dedupe_ratio_baseline dedupe_ratio_drift_pct target_min_dedupe_ratio below_min_dedupe severity_bucket freeze_active owner_team policy_gap_reason
```

Step 3 — Validate

Open the SimpliVity HTML5 plug-in inside vSphere Client and navigate SimpliVity, Backups, All Backups; choose a VM you know is protected and compare its last successful state and timestamp to the newest simplivity:backup:job event fields state and created_at in Splunk for the same virtual_machine_id. Move to Backup Policies and confirm frequency_minutes and retention_minutes match the latest simplivity:policy event for the same policy id; if labels differ from policy_name text, trust ids first. Open Cluster, Storage Efficiency and compare dedupe_ratio to simplivity:cluster:efficiency; if efficiency_ratio is absent on your OmniStack release, omit it from capacity panels rather than forcing zero through a bad extraction.

From the OVC shell, use omnistack-cli (exact verbs per your support contract) to show a known backup object and compare identifiers and state to the HEC events after the next poll cycle. If CLI access is blocked, schedule a controlled lab backup of a throwaway VM, wait for completion, and verify the since cursor advances in poller logs without skipping pages. This step is intentionally SimpliVity-native and is not a multi-site async replication drill, not a vSAN resync review, and not a storage replication-factor audit.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard layout: top row single value of policies with severity_bucket in critical; second single value of VM gap rows; third a timechart of failure_pct by policy_display; fourth a table of policy rows sorted by failure_pct with rpo_breach flag; fifth a cluster-level strip chart of dedupe_ratio_drift_pct. Add drilldowns that open the SimpliVity plug-in deep link bookmark your portal team maintains.

Runbooks: for failed backup bursts, confirm datastore and destination health, restart failed jobs from the UI after vendor guidance, and attach Splunk excerpts with policy_display. For rpo_breach on policy rows, page owner_team from simplivity_policy_owner.csv and include oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes versus target_rpo_minutes. For vm_gap, assign virtualization owners to bind the right policy or document an intentional exclusion with CMDB tagging so you can filter vm_gap. For dedupe_ratio_drift_pct negative trend, open a capacity review because logical efficiency changed; tie outcomes to procurement rather than assuming silent data loss.

Relate this dashboard to UC-19.2.31 only as the Nutanix disaster-recovery analog for organizations that operate both platforms; relate to UC-19.2.6 only as a reminder that data replica counts do not replace backup outcomes. Ownership sits with Head of IT Operations for paging policy, backup operations for first response, and storage planning for dedupe regression.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

1) OAuth token expired or invalid_grant storm: service password rotated without updating vault; fix credentials, restart poller, verify POST /api/oauth/token returns 200, then verify first GET /api/backups succeeds.

2) Since-cursor advanced while Splunk indexers were offline: widen the next poll to since equals twenty-four hours ago once, accept duplicate backup ids for that window, dedup on id field in a separate data-quality search if required.

3) Policy renamed in SimpliVity UI mid-week: ids remain stable; refresh simplivity_policy_owner.csv policy_name column for human dashboards; joins continue to work on policy_id.

4) Orphan VM is acceptable: templates, GPU lab instances without backup contract, or disposable automation clones should be tagged out of vm_gap via an exceptions lookup keyed on vm_id if your governance allows; do not silence the whole panel.

5) Cluster dedupe_ratio reads zero after OVC service bounce: wait for OmniStack efficiency services to finish initialization; compare CLI or HTML5 Storage Efficiency after ten to fifteen minutes before declaring regression.

6) OVC failover during poll: poller may see HTTP 503 or empty objects arrays for one interval; rely on retry with backoff and confirm the next five-minute sample restores non-zero counts before paging; optional vmware:vc:event can show correlated vCenter tasks without replacing SimpliVity as the source of truth.

Extended note: if VMware TA is present, add a correlated panel only; keep this saved search paste-and-run without mandatory vmware:vc:event to avoid index dependency sprawl for greenfield SimpliVity-only estates.

Final checklist: simplivity_policy_owner.csv columns verified, HEC ACK on, props consistent on indexers, and RBAC for hci separates operators from executives reading summaries.

references.title: Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector
HPE Support — SimpliVity and OmniStack documentation

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — KPIs for percent policies under RPO and open vm_gap count

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab OVC, ingest simplivity:backup:job events where twenty percent of synthetic jobs are failed for a governed policy row present in simplivity_policy_owner.csv with target_rpo_minutes tighter than the observed oldest_successful_backup_age_minutes; the saved search should emit high or critical severity_bucket for row_kind=policy within the scheduled window, and separately ingest a simplivity:vm row with empty policy_id to produce vm_gap at warning.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During an active change_freeze_until window, the same breach signal should demote critical to warning and high to info in severity_bucket while freeze_active=1, and a steady cluster with dedupe_ratio within five percent of its seven-day average should remain info when failure_pct is zero and no vm_gap rows exist.
